notes.content: Every living organism, from the smallest and most primitive bacteria to every plant, insect, animal or human being, contains DNA as the primary genetic material. DNA directs all cellular processes, creating the incredible variety and diversity of living organisms in the biosphere. Biotechnology focuses on the mechanics of life processes and their application. Biotechnology means "life technology" and represents an integrated, multidisciplinary field, with a profound impact today on almost every aspect of human endeavor.
notes.contentHtml: Every living organism, from the smallest and most primitive bacteria to every plant, insect, animal or human being, contains DNA as the primary genetic material. DNA directs all cellular processes, creating the incredible variety and diversity of living organisms in the biosphere. Biotechnology focuses on the mechanics of life processes and their application. Biotechnology means "life technology" and represents an integrated, multidisciplinary field, with a profound impact today on almost every aspect of human endeavor.

notes.content: This is a competitive major and is subject to the Selective Major Review process. Students need to take all courses for a letter grade with no grade less than C and complete all major preparatory course requirements by the end of spring term prior to their fall enrollment. All students must meet a 2.8 GPA cumulative GPA for admission. Complete the courses below and maintain a 2.50 GPA in each series: ○ Biological Sciences (BIS) 002A/B/C ○ Chemistry (CHE) 002A/B/C ○ Mathematics (MAT) 017A/B or 021A/B Visit Department agreements as needed Contact your UC Davis Advisor for questions ( Find my advisor ) Note: Advanced Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) Examination: AP and IB examination credit policies are detailed in the UC Davis General Catalog . Quick reference charts for AP and IB .
notes.contentHtml:
This is a competitive major and is subject to the Selective Major Review process. Students need to take all courses for a letter grade with no grade less than C and complete all major preparatory course requirements by the end of spring term prior to their fall enrollment. All students must meet a 2.8 GPA cumulative GPA for admission.


Complete the courses below and maintain a 2.50 GPA in each series:


○    Biological Sciences (BIS) 002A/B/C

○    Chemistry (CHE) 002A/B/C

○    Mathematics (MAT) 017A/B or 021A/B


Visit Department agreements as needed


Contact your UC Davis Advisor for questions (Find my advisor)


Note:

Advanced Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) Examination:

AP and IB examination credit policies are detailed in the UC Davis General Catalog. Quick reference charts for AP and IB.

notes.content: At the time of TAG submission, Biotechnology requires a minimum 3.20 cumulative GPA and at least a 2.50 GPA in each series with no grades less than a C to receive a Transfer Admission Guarantee.
notes.contentHtml: At the time of TAG submission, Biotechnology requires a minimum 3.20 cumulative GPA and at least a 2.50 GPA in each series with no grades less than a C to receive a Transfer Admission Guarantee.
